exercises.title: DIGIT COLLAPSE
exercises.brief: Crush a number down to a single glowing digit.
promptLines: Define **digitalRoot(n: number): number** — keep replacing `n` with the sum of its digits until only one digit is left. Assume `n >= 0`.
starterLines: // TODO: sum digits repeatedly until one digit remains
exercises.hint: Outer loop while n >= 10; inner loop adds n % 10 and does n = Math.floor(n / 10).
exercises.lore: Fold the number in on itself until a single rune remains.

exercises.title: SYNC CYCLE
exercises.brief: When do the two cycles realign?
promptLines: Define **lcm(a: number, b: number): number** — the least common multiple of two positive integers (a*b divided by their gcd).
starterLines: function lcm(a: number, b: number): number {
solutionLines: function lcm(a: number, b: number): number {
function gcd(x: number, y: number): number { return y === 0 ? x : gcd(y, x % y); }

exercises.title: BINARY BEACON
exercises.brief: Does this number sit exactly on a power-of-two boundary?
promptLines: Define **isPowerOfTwo(n: number): boolean** — `true` when `n` is `1, 2, 4, 8, 16, ...`, and `false` for `0`, negatives, and anything in between.
starterLines: // TODO: only exact powers of two should pass
exercises.hint: A power of two has a single set bit: n > 0 && (n & (n - 1)) === 0.
exercises.lore: One lone bit burning in the dark — that is a true beacon.

exercises.title: PRECISION GATE
exercises.brief: Snap a value to a fixed number of decimal places.
promptLines: Define **roundTo(n: number, places: number): number** — round `n` to `places` decimal places.
Scale up, round, scale back down: `Math.round(n * 10 ** places) / 10 ** places`.
starterLines: function roundTo(n: number, places: number): number {
solutionLines: function roundTo(n: number, places: number): number {
exercises.lore: Trim the noise; keep only the decimals that matter.

exercises.title: FACTOR HARVEST
exercises.brief: Add up every divisor a number will admit.
promptLines: Define **sumDivisors(n: number): number** — the sum of every positive divisor of `n`, including `1` and `n` itself. Assume `n >= 1`.
starterLines: // TODO: add every d from 1..n that divides n
solutionLines: for (let d: number = 1; d <= n; d++) {
exercises.hint: Loop d from 1 to n; whenever n % d === 0, add d to a running total.
exercises.lore: Lay out every factor the number will surrender, then weigh the pile.
